body.p: MarkLogic offers a very sophisticated permissions and roles system.ConceptsDocument Permissions Since we primarily use MarkLogic as a database and not as a Content Management System, it is sufficient to know the basic functionality.

note.p: Without further measures, documents are assigned the permissions of their creator.

body.p: If you load documents into the database via the MarkLogic Content Pump, for example with this command:
body.pre:
mlcp_opts="-database alex-test -host localhost -username admin -password admin"
mlcp import $mlcp_opts \ -output_permissions xml-scrapper,read,xml-scrapper,update -input_file_path input-files \ -input_file_type aggregates \ -aggregate_record_element chapter \ -output_collections /chapter \ -output_uri_prefix /chapter/ \ -output_uri_suffix .xml
body.p: It is particularly important to set the option:
if, for example, you only provide a single user with a specific role for a web app.

messagepanel.typeofhazard: If you forget the [[code:-output_permissions]] option, you may not be able to display the documents from a web app.
messagepanel.howtoavoid: In this case, no permissions are set on the uploaded documents. You can then only download them in a web app when logged in as an admin.

body.p: There is a simple way to check the permissions on a document. To do this, you run the "Browse" action on a database in the console and switch to the Permissions tab:

fig.title: Display of Document Permissions in the MarkLogic Database
fig.desc: The "Browse" button in the MarkLogic console is often overlooked, but offers many useful features, such as displaying the set document permissions.

messagepanel.typeofhazard: An XQuery script that runs a web app can not only be called in a browser, but can also be accessed by an external program. For this to work, the permissions of the web app must be set as described in chapter Wikipedia Scraper Application.
messagepanel.howtoavoid: Ideally, you should also assign a role [[nb:xml-scrapper]] to the user [[nb:xml-scrapper]], which can only read and modify documents with the permissions [[nb:-output_permissions xml-scrapper,read,xml-scrapper,update]], cf. above — and not the admin role.
